metadata.title: Anomalous deaths & disappearances of figures in strategically sensitive domains (Russian energy/finance executives; nuclear scientists; Chinese defense-tech researchers; aerospace whistleblowers; crypto figures)
metadata.frame: A deliberately DISCIPLINED treatment of a topic that is a magnet for conspiracy. The honest finding is NOT 'a single hidden hand is killing everyone.' It is that several DISTINCT clusters exist, each with its own (very different) evidentiary status: some are formally attributed state assassinations (Iranian nuclear scientists), some are officially ruled suicide/accident but contested by families/observers (Boeing whistleblowers), some are statistically striking clusters with NO established cause or perpetrator (Russian energy executives; Chinese defense scientists), and some are widely-hyped online but largely explained by base rates and coincidence (most 'crypto deaths'). Each case is graded by attribution quality. No unified campaign is asserted; base-rate and clustering-illusion caveats are stated explicitly. Source-pointer layer: no foul play is attributed to any named person or state beyond what an authoritative source already states.
metadata.relevance: Ties to existing legs: Russian energy deaths -> [[macro-history-dereg-manipulation]] / Russia-energy-arctic; nuclear-scientist killings -> defense/non-proliferation; Chinese defense-tech/REE researchers -> [[geopolitics-chip-chokepoint-war]] / [[spec-rare-earth-statecraft]] / Taiwan; aerospace whistleblowers -> disclosure & accountability ([[macro-official-data-integrity]]); crypto figures -> [[spec-onchain-threat-actor-addresses]] context.
metadata.sources: Meduza, Reuters, BBC, AP, NYT, Wikipedia (Suspicious deaths of Russian businesspeople 2022-2023), Charleston County Coroner, NPR, Seattle Times, RFE/RL, Times of Israel, USIP Iran Primer, Newsweek, South China Morning Post / Yahoo News, US House Select Committee on the CCP. Full URLs in the rendered .md.

base_rate_caveat.summary: Before any cluster reads as sinister, the statistical floor must be stated.

points.detail: There are thousands of senior energy executives in Russia, thousands of crypto founders, tens of thousands of defense-affiliated scientists in China. In any population that large, a steady stream of deaths (heart attacks, cancer, accidents, suicides) is statistically expected. A list of N striking cases is meaningless without the base rate and the denominator.

Lists are curated AFTER the fact to include only deaths that 'look' suspicious (young, sudden, prominent), and exclude the ordinary majority. Humans over-detect patterns in random clustering. This is survivorship/selection bias, not evidence of a plot.

A cluster becomes evidentially serious when it shows: (a) a common, specific antecedent (e.g., all had just testified, all worked one program), (b) implausible cause-of-death mechanics, (c) official attribution, or (d) a credible actor with motive AND demonstrated capability. These are the tests applied per-strand below.

strand_russia_energy_finance.summary: The most statistically striking civilian cluster: a sustained run of sudden deaths among Russian energy/finance executives since the 2022 invasion. Pattern is real and documented; a unifying CAUSE is not established.

points.detail: Independent outlet Meduza (Jul 2025) reviewed the sudden deaths of ~13 Russian energy and transportation executives since 2022; broader tallies of 'suspicious deaths of Russian businesspeople' run higher. Companies touched include Gazprom, Lukoil, Transneft, Novatek, Gazprombank.

Ravil Maganov (Lukoil chairman) — fell from a hospital window, Sep 2022. Leonid Shulman & Alexander Tyulyakov (Gazprom units) — found dead, Jan/Feb 2022. Vladislav Avayev (Gazprombank VP) — murder-suicide per official account, Apr 2022. Vladimir Nekrasov (Lukoil chairman) — 'acute heart failure', Oct 2023. Andrei Badalov (Transneft VP) — fell from a window, Jul 2025.
points.grade: fact (deaths/dates); CAUSE per-case ranges from official ruling to unexplained

points.detail: No official body has attributed these to a coordinated campaign. Hypotheses in reporting span: Kremlin/elite enforcement, sanctions-related financial ruin and suicide, organized-crime/business disputes, and genuine coincidence. Russian official causes (heart failure, suicide, accident) are widely distrusted given the information environment but are NOT independently disproven.

strand_nuclear_scientists.summary: The one strand with FORMAL attribution to state action: the multi-year targeted killing of Iranian nuclear scientists, widely attributed to Israel.

points.detail: Iran's top nuclear scientist, assassinated near Tehran 27 Nov 2020 using a remote-operated, AI-assisted machine gun (per NYT reporting). Described as the program's 'brains.'
points.grade: fact (killing); attribution to Israel widely reported, not officially claimed at the time

points.detail: Fakhrizadeh was at least the fifth Iranian nuclear-linked scientist killed or dead in suspicious circumstances since ~2007 — incl. Masoud Ali Mohammadi (2010, motorcycle bomb), Majid Shahriari (2010), Darioush Rezaeinejad (2011), Mostafa Ahmadi Roshan (2012, Natanz supervisor, magnetic car bomb).

Subsequent Israel-Iran escalation reportedly included further killings of nuclear/scientific personnel. This strand is a genuine, decades-long covert-action campaign — the clearest case where 'scientists keep dying' has a documented mechanism and attributed actor.

strand_china_defense_tech_scientists.summary: A reported cluster of sudden deaths/disappearances among Chinese defense, microelectronics, and military-AI researchers — striking but with NO established cause or perpetrator, and explicitly NOT linked to other clusters.

points.detail: Outlets (incl. Newsweek, SCMP-sourced) flagged ~10 Chinese scientists dying in suspicious circumstances, several in 2023, working on drones, military AI, microelectronics, and Taiwan-invasion simulations. Examples cited: Feng Yanghe (defense-tech professor, car crash, Jul 2023, reportedly working on Taiwan-conflict simulations); Zhou Guangyuan (chemist, died at 51, Dec 2023, no cause given); Zhang Xiaoxin (space expert, reported car accident, Dec 2024).

Separately, senior PRC tech-defense officials have been detained in anti-corruption / loyalty purges — e.g., Chen Zhaoxiong (ex-chairman, China Electronics Technology Group) taken by investigators, 2024. This is documented state action of a DIFFERENT kind (internal purge), not unexplained death.
points.grade: fact (detentions); distinct from the death cluster

points.detail: Reporting explicitly states there is NO evidence linking the Chinese cases to US cases or to each other. Plausible explanations include genuine accidents/illness, internal security actions, and selection bias. Treat as an open, unexplained cluster.

strand_aerospace_whistleblowers.summary: Two Boeing-linked whistleblower deaths in 2024 — officially ruled suicide and infection respectively, but the TIMING (mid-litigation) drives sustained suspicion.

points.detail: Former Boeing quality manager, found dead 9 Mar 2024 in Charleston, SC, the day after deposition in his retaliation suit. The Charleston County Coroner and police concluded (reports released 17 May 2024) death by self-inflicted gunshot; records note PTSD/anxiety. Family and his lawyers expressed disbelief given timing.
points.grade: fact (official ruling: suicide); contested by family on timing

points.detail: Spirit AeroSystems quality auditor and Boeing-737-MAX whistleblower; died 1 May 2024 (age 45) of a fast-spreading infection (reported MRSA) after sudden illness. His mother requested an autopsy.
points.grade: fact (illness death); no evidence of foul play

points.detail: Two deaths of related whistleblowers within ~2 months is a striking coincidence that fuels suspicion, but the official causes are independently documented (coroner; medical) and no evidence of foul play has emerged. The substantive accountability issue is the underlying safety/retaliation allegations, which stand on their own regardless of the deaths.

strand_crypto_figures.summary: The most over-hyped strand: a run of crypto-figure deaths (2022-2023) heavily amplified online, but largely explained by base rates and coincidence, with little-to-no foul-play evidence in most cases.

points.detail: Nikolai Mushegian (MakerDAO/DAI co-architect) — drowned, Puerto Rico, 28 Oct 2022, hours after a paranoid tweet alleging threats. Tiantian Kullander (Amber Group co-founder) — died in his sleep at 30, Nov 2022. Vyacheslav Taran (Forex/crypto) — helicopter crash, Nov 2022. Others (Javier Biosca, Fernando Pérez Algaba) involved crime/violence unrelated to any unified theory.
points.grade: fact (deaths); foul play NOT established in most

points.detail: Newsweek and others note there is limited-to-no evidence these resulted from a plot. Causes span drowning, natural death, and an aviation accident — heterogeneous, not a signature. This strand best illustrates the base-rate/clustering caveat: a large young-founder population produces tragic deaths that retro-fit a narrative.

synthesis.summary: Five clusters, five different truths — the value is in REFUSING to collapse them into one.

points.detail: From strongest to weakest: (1) Iranian nuclear scientists = documented, attributed state assassination campaign. (2) Russian energy executives = real, sustained, statistically striking cluster, cause unestablished. (3) Boeing whistleblowers = official causes documented, suspicion driven by timing. (4) Chinese defense scientists = reported cluster, circumstances unverified, no perpetrator. (5) Crypto figures = largely base-rate coincidence.

Each cluster intersects a project leg (energy, non-proliferation, chip/REE statecraft, corporate accountability, crypto). The disciplined point is methodological: 'they keep disappearing' is a pattern-claim that must be decomposed and graded, not asserted as a single conspiracy. This block models that decomposition.

checks.result: Meduza (Jul 2025) reviewed ~13 energy/transport execs since 2022; Wikipedia 'Suspicious deaths of Russian businesspeople (2022-2023)' compiles the broader list with per-case sourcing.
Charleston County Coroner + police, reports released 17 May 2024: self-inflicted gunshot; PTSD/anxiety noted.
Reported MRSA / fast-spreading infection; autopsy requested by family (Seattle Times/NPR).
27 Nov 2020, remote/AI-assisted weapon (NYT); >=5th Iranian nuclear scientist killed since 2007 (RFE/RL, USIP).
Reporting explicitly states NO evidence links Chinese cases to US cases or to each other (Newsweek/Yahoo).
Newsweek: limited-to-no evidence of a plot; causes heterogeneous (drowning/natural/aviation).

what_is_NOT_asserted.purpose: Explicit non-claims, to prevent inferring a unified conspiracy nobody has evidenced.
non_claims: It is NOT asserted that a single actor or coordinated campaign is responsible across these clusters.
It is NOT asserted that the Russian-executive or Chinese-scientist deaths were murders — only that they form unexplained clusters whose official causes are distrusted but not disproven.

It is NOT asserted that Boeing or any party caused the whistleblower deaths — the official causes are suicide (Barnett) and infection (Dean); the safety/retaliation allegations are the substantive issue and stand independently.
It is NOT asserted that the crypto deaths were foul play — most are best explained by base rates and coincidence.
No private individual or state is accused beyond what an authoritative source (court/coroner/government/major outlet) already states.
These edges are OVERLAY/context only and are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: attributed targeted-killing campaign of nuclear scientists (Fakhrizadeh 2020 + >=4 since 2007); widely reported, not officially claimed
edges.note: OVERLAY: documented/attributed state action; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: unexplained sustained death cluster since 2022 (Lukoil/Gazprom/Transneft); NO established perpetrator
edges.note: OVERLAY: unexplained cluster, cause unestablished; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: two whistleblower deaths during litigation, 2024 (Barnett ruled suicide; Dean infection); underlying retaliation/safety claims independent
edges.note: OVERLAY: official causes documented; suspicion is timing-driven; excluded from proofs

limits: This is a SOURCE-POINTER + GRADING layer, not an accusation. Every cluster is decomposed and graded by attribution quality; the strongest (Iranian scientists) is a documented attributed campaign, the weakest (crypto) is largely coincidence. Base-rate and clustering-illusion caveats are stated up front. Official causes (coroner/medical/government) are recorded even where distrusted, and not overridden absent contrary evidence. No unified conspiracy is asserted. Overlay edges are excluded from the formal proofs. source: see verification_log and the rendered .md for full URLs.
